background: Let us return to the birth weight illustrative data concerning 10 SIDS cases. We have established that \bar{x}=2890.5 grams and s=720.0.

task.query: Confidence intervals for \mu at 90 \% level of confidence will be calculated.
task.answer: The standard error of the mean SE_{\bar{x}}=\frac{720}{\sqrt{10}}=227.684. df=10-1=9. For 90 \% confidence, use t_{9,95}=1.833 [Table C], so the 90 \% confidence interval for \mu is \bar{x} \pm\left(t_{n-1,1-\xi}\right)\left(SE_{\bar{x}}\right)=2890.5 \pm (1.833)(227.684)=2890.5 \pm 417.3=(2473.2 to 3307.8) grams.

task.query: Confidence intervals for \mu at 95 \% level of confidence will be calculated.
task.answer: For 95 \% confidence, use t_{9, .975}=2.262, so the 95 \% confidence interval for \mu is 2890.5 \pm(2.262)(227.684)=2890.5 \pm 515.1= (2375.4 to 3405.6) grams.

task.query: Confidence intervals for \mu at 99 \% level of confidence will be calculated.
task.answer: For 99 \% confidence, use t_{9, .995}=3.250, so the 99 \% confidence interval for \mu is 2890.5 \pm(3.250)(227.7)=2890.5 \pm 740.0= (2150.5 to 3630.5) grams.
